6	Spring Boot helps you to create Spring-powered, production-grade applications and services with absolute minimum fuss.
7	It takes an opinionated view of the Spring platform so that new and existing users can quickly get to the bits they need.
8	
9	You can use Spring Boot to create stand-alone Java applications that can be started using `java -jar` or more traditional WAR deployments.
10	We also provide a command-line tool that runs Spring scripts.
11	
12	Our primary goals are:
13	
14	* Provide a radically faster and widely accessible getting started experience for all Spring development.
15	* Be opinionated, but get out of the way quickly as requirements start to diverge from the defaults.
16	* Provide a range of non-functional features common to large classes of projects (for example, embedded servers, security, metrics, health checks, externalized configuration).
17	* Absolutely no code generation and no requirement for XML configuration.
18	
19	
20	
21	== Installation and Getting Started
22	
23	The {docs}[reference documentation] includes detailed {docs}/installing.html[installation instructions] as well as a comprehensive {docs}/tutorial/first-application/index.html[``getting started``] guide.
24	
25	Here is a quick teaser of a complete Spring Boot application in Java:
26	
27	[source,java]
28	----
29	import org.springframework.boot.*;
30	import org.springframework.boot.autoconfigure.*;
31	import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.*;
32	
33	@RestController
34	@SpringBootApplication
35	public class Example {
36	
37		@RequestMapping("/")
38		String home() {
39			return "Hello World!";
40		}
41	
42		public static void main(String[] args) {
43			SpringApplication.run(Example.class, args);
44		}
45	
46	}
47	----

51	== Getting Help
52	
53	Are you having trouble with Spring Boot? We want to help!
54	
55	* Check the {docs}/[reference documentation], especially the {docs}/how-to/index.html[How-to's] -- they provide solutions to the most common questions.
56	* Learn the Spring basics -- Spring Boot builds on many other Spring projects; check the https://spring.io[spring.io] website for a wealth of reference documentation.
57	  If you are new to Spring, try one of the https://spring.io/guides[guides].
58	* If you are upgrading, read the {github}/wiki[release notes] for upgrade instructions and "new and noteworthy" features.
59	* Ask a question -- we monitor https://stackoverflow.com[stackoverflow.com] for questions tagged with https://stackoverflow.com/tags/spring-boot[`spring-boot`].
60	* Report bugs with Spring Boot at {github}/issues[github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/issues].
61	
62	
63	
64	== Contributing
65	
66	We welcome contributions of all kinds!
67	Please read our link:CONTRIBUTING.adoc[contribution guidelines] before submitting a pull request.
68	
69	
70	
71	== Reporting Issues
72	
73	Spring Boot uses GitHub's integrated issue tracking system to record bugs and feature requests.
74	If you want to raise an issue, please follow the recommendations below:
75	
76	* Before you log a bug, please search the {github}/issues[issue tracker] to see if someone has already reported the problem.
77	* If the issue doesn't already exist, {github}/issues/new[create a new issue].
78	* Please provide as much information as possible with the issue report.
79	We like to know the Spring Boot version, operating system, and JVM version you're using.
80	* If you need to paste code or include a stack trace, use Markdown.
81	+++```+++ escapes before and after your text.
82	* If possible, try to create a test case or project that replicates the problem and attach it to the issue.
83	
84	
85	
86	== Building from Source
87	
88	You don't need to build from source to use Spring Boot.
89	If you want to try out the latest and greatest, Spring Boot can be built and published to your local Maven cache using the https://docs.gradle.org/current/userguide/gradle_wrapper.html[Gradle wrapper].
90	You also need JDK 25.
91	
92	[source,shell]
93	----
94	$ ./gradlew publishToMavenLocal
95	----
96	
97	This command builds all modules and publishes them to your local Maven cache.
98	It won't run any of the tests.
99	If you want to build everything, use the `build` task:
100	
101	[source,shell]
102	----
103	$ ./gradlew build
104	----
105	
106	
107	
108	== Guides
109	
110	The https://spring.io/[spring.io] site contains several guides that show how to use Spring Boot step-by-step:
111	
112	* https://spring.io/guides/gs/spring-boot/[Building an Application with Spring Boot] is an introductory guide that shows you how to create an application, run it, and add some management services.
113	* https://spring.io/guides/gs/actuator-service/[Building a RESTful Web Service with Spring Boot Actuator] is a guide to creating a REST web service and also shows how the server can be configured.
114	
115	
116	
117	== License
118	
119	Spring Boot is Open Source software released under the https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0.html[Apache 2.0 license].

25	import org.springframework.core.env.ConfigurableEnvironment;
26	import org.springframework.core.env.Environment;
27	
28	/**
29	 * Strategy interface for creating the {@link ConfigurableApplicationContext} used by a
30	 * {@link SpringApplication}. Created contexts should be returned in their default form,
31	 * with the {@code SpringApplication} responsible for configuring and refreshing the
32	 * context.
33	 *
34	 * @author Andy Wilkinson
35	 * @author Phillip Webb
36	 * @since 2.4.0
37	 */
38	@FunctionalInterface
39	public interface ApplicationContextFactory {
40	
41		/**
42		 * A default {@link ApplicationContextFactory} implementation that will create an
43		 * appropriate context for the {@link WebApplicationType}.
44		 */
45		ApplicationContextFactory DEFAULT = new DefaultApplicationContextFactory();
46	
47		/**
48		 * Return the {@link Environment} type expected to be set on the
49		 * {@link #create(WebApplicationType) created} application context. The result of this
50		 * method can be used to convert an existing environment instance to the correct type.
51		 * @param webApplicationType the web application type or {@code null}
52		 * @return the expected application context type or {@code null} to use the default
53		 * @since 2.6.14
54		 */
55		default @Nullable Class<? extends ConfigurableEnvironment> getEnvironmentType(
56				@Nullable WebApplicationType webApplicationType) {
57			return null;
58		}
59	
60		/**
61		 * Create a new {@link Environment} to be set on the
62		 * {@link #create(WebApplicationType) created} application context. The result of this
63		 * method must match the type returned by
64		 * {@link #getEnvironmentType(WebApplicationType)}.
65		 * @param webApplicationType the web application type or {@code null}
66		 * @return an environment instance or {@code null} to use the default
67		 * @since 2.6.14
68		 */
69		default @Nullable ConfigurableEnvironment createEnvironment(@Nullable WebApplicationType webApplicationType) {
70			return null;
71		}
72	
73		/**
74		 * Creates the {@link ConfigurableApplicationContext application context} for a
75		 * {@link SpringApplication}, respecting the given {@code webApplicationType}.
76		 * @param webApplicationType the web application type
77		 * @return the newly created application context
78		 */
79		@Nullable ConfigurableApplicationContext create(@Nullable WebApplicationType webApplicationType);
80	
81		/**
82		 * Creates an {@code ApplicationContextFactory} that will create contexts by
83		 * instantiating the given {@code contextClass} through its primary constructor.
84		 * @param contextClass the context class
85		 * @return the factory that will instantiate the context class
86		 * @see BeanUtils#instantiateClass(Class)
87		 */
88		static ApplicationContextFactory ofContextClass(Class<? extends ConfigurableApplicationContext> contextClass) {
89			return of(() -> BeanUtils.instantiateClass(contextClass));
90		}
91	
92		/**
93		 * Creates an {@code ApplicationContextFactory} that will create contexts by calling
94		 * the given {@link Supplier}.
95		 * @param supplier the context supplier, for example
96		 * {@code AnnotationConfigApplicationContext::new}
97		 * @return the factory that will instantiate the context class
98		 */
99		static ApplicationContextFactory of(Supplier<ConfigurableApplicationContext> supplier) {
100			return (webApplicationType) -> supplier.get();
101		}
102	
103	}

## Summary of Spring Application Context Refresh Lifecycle

Based on my thorough search of the Spring Boot repository at `/home/bench/repos/spring-boot`, here's what I found:

### Spring Boot Files Found:

1. **SpringApplication.refresh() entry point:**
   - File: `/home/bench/repos/spring-boot/core/spring-boot/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/SpringApplication.java`
   - Lines: 755-757 (refresh method delegates to context.refresh())
   - Line: 441-446 (refreshContext method that calls refresh)
   - Line: 304-342 (run method orchestration)

   - File: `/home/bench/repos/spring-boot/core/spring-boot-autoconfigure/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/autoconfigure/AutoConfigurationImportSelector.java`
   - Line: 78-79 (class declaration)

   - Line: 119-125 (selectImports method)
   - Line: 142-155 (getAutoConfigurationEntry - main orchestration)

3. **DefaultBootstrapContext:**
   - File: `/home/bench/repos/spring-boot/core/spring-boot/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/bootstrap/DefaultBootstrapContext.java`
   - Lines: 38-90 (full implementation)

4. **ApplicationContextFactory:**
   - File: `/home/bench/repos/spring-boot/core/spring-boot/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/ApplicationContextFactory.java`
   - Lines: 38-103 (interface and factory method implementations)

5. **DefaultApplicationContextFactory:**
   - File: `/home/bench/repos/spring-boot/core/spring-boot/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/DefaultApplicationContextFactory.java`
   - Line: 71 (creates AnnotationConfigApplicationContext)
   - Line: 73 (creates GenericApplicationContext for AOT)

### Spring Framework Classes (External Dependency - NOT in Spring Boot repo):

The following classes are from Spring Framework and must be sourced from the Spring Framework repository (https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework):

1. **AbstractApplicationContext.refresh()** - Spring Framework org.springframework.context.support package
   - This is the core method that orchestrates the entire bean lifecycle
   - Contains all the steps: obtainFreshBeanFactory, prepareBeanFactory, postProcessBeanFactory, etc.

2. **ConfigurableApplicationContext** - Spring Framework org.springframework.context package
   - Interface that AbstractApplicationContext implements

3. **GenericApplicationContext** - Spring Framework org.springframework.context.support package
   - Concrete implementation used by Spring Boot

4. **AnnotationConfigApplicationContext** - Spring Framework org.springframework.context.annotation package
   - Default context used by Spring Boot for non-web applications

5. **AnnotationConfigUtils.registerAnnotationConfigProcessors()** - Spring Framework org.springframework.context.annotation package
   - Registers ConfigurationClassPostProcessor and other annotation processors

6. **ConfigurationClassPostProcessor** - Spring Framework org.springframework.context.annotation package
   - Key BeanFactoryPostProcessor that processes @Configuration classes

7. **DefaultListableBeanFactory.preInstantiateSingletons()** - Spring Framework org.springframework.beans.factory.support package
   - Core bean instantiation loop

8. **AbstractBeanFactory.getBean()/doGetBean()** - Spring Framework org.springframework.beans.factory.support package
   - Bean retrieval logic

9. **AbstractAutowireCapableBeanFactory.createBean()/doCreateBean()** - Spring Framework org.springframework.beans.factory.support package
   - Bean creation logic

### Important Finding:

At line 735-737 of SpringApplication.java, there's a cast to `AbstractApplicationContext`:
```java
if (context instanceof AbstractApplicationContext abstractApplicationContext) {
    return (BeanDefinitionRegistry) abstractApplicationContext.getBeanFactory();
}
```

This confirms that Spring Boot works with Spring Framework's AbstractApplicationContext.

To get the exact line numbers and implementations for the Spring Framework classes, you would need to access the Spring Framework repository directly, as they are not included in the Spring Boot repository.

Similar to the {@link ServletContext#addFilter(String, Filter) registration} 28 * features provided by {@link ServletContext} but with a Spring Bean friendly design. 29 *

30 * The {@link #setFilter(Filter) Filter} must be specified before calling 31 * {@link #onStartup(ServletContext)}. Registrations can be associated with 32 * {@link #setUrlPatterns URL patterns} and/or servlets (either by {@link #setServletNames 33 * name} or through a {@link #setServletRegistrationBeans ServletRegistrationBean}s). When 34 * no URL pattern or servlets are specified the filter will be associated to '/*'. The 35 * filter name will be deduced if not specified. 36 * 37 * @param the type of {@link Filter} to register 38 * @author Phillip Webb 39 * @since 1.4.0 40 * @see ServletContextInitializer 41 * @see ServletContext#addFilter(String, Filter) 42 * @see DelegatingFilterProxyRegistrationBean 43 * @see FilterRegistration 44 */ 45 public class FilterRegistrationBean extends AbstractFilterRegistrationBean { 46 47 private @Nullable T filter; 48 49 /** 50 * Create a new {@link FilterRegistrationBean} instance. 51 */ 52 public FilterRegistrationBean() { 53 } 54 55 /** 56 * Create a new {@link FilterRegistrationBean} instance to be registered with the 57 * specified {@link ServletRegistrationBean}s. 58 * @param filter the filter to register 59 * @param servletRegistrationBeans associate {@link ServletRegistrationBean}s 60 */ 61 public FilterRegistrationBean(T filter, ServletRegistrationBean... servletRegistrationBeans) { 62 super(servletRegistrationBeans); 63 Assert.notNull(filter, "'filter' must not be null"); 64 this.filter = filter; 65 } 66 67 @Override 68 public @Nullable T getFilter() { 69 return this.filter; 70 } 71 72 /** 73 * Set the filter to be registered. 74 * @param filter the filter 75 */ 76 public void setFilter(T filter) { 77 Assert.notNull(filter, "'filter' must not be null"); 78 this.filter = filter; 79 } 80 81 } 82
